memoryArtifacts.description: Origin Displayed: English - Spelling variations of this family name include: Purefoy, Purefield, Purefree, Purefrey, Purfrey, Purfry and many more. First found in Leicestershire where they were seated from very early times and were granted lands by Duke William of Normandy, their liege Lord, for their distinguished assistance at the Battle of Hastings in 1066 A.D. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Lieutenant Purfrey who settled in Virginia in 1623; Lucy Purefoy, who settled in Virginia in 1629; Samuell Purefoy, who came to St. Christopher (New England) in 1633. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009).

zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabella Puresey於1472年出生在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾時，她的父親John Purefoy24歲，母親Joan Wistowe30歲。她於1492年與John Sutton在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1498年享年26歲。

nameMeanings.name: Puresey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Percy
meanings: nickname from Old French percehaie ‘pierce hedge’ (Old French percer ‘to pierce, penetrate’ + haie ‘hedge, fence’), perhaps with the sense of someone breaking into an enclosure. Percehaie is the name of one of the sons of the foxes Renart and Hermeline in the medieval French epic Roman de Renart, whose earliest known version is from the 1170s. The surname is older than that, but it may originate in a nickname for the fox as ‘enclosure piercer’, perhaps amounting to ‘chicken thief’.

habitational name from any of several places called Percy in Calvados, Eure, and Manche; William de Perci, the Domesday tenant-in-chief and under-tenant of Hugh, Earl of Chester, came from either Percy-en-Auge (Eure) or Percy (Manche).

nameMeanings.name: Isabella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabella Puresey was born in 1472, in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Purefoy, was 24 and her mother, Joan Wistowe, was 30. She married John Sutton in 1492, in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1498, at the age of 26.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabella Puresey est née en 1472, à Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Purefoy, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Joan Wistowe, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé John Sutton en 1492, à Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1498, à l’âge de 26 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabella Puresey 1472 in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Purefoy 24 und ihre Mutter Joan Wistowe 30. Sie hat 1492 in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom John Sutton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1498 im Alter von 26 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabella Puresey è nata il nel 1472 nel Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John Purefoy, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Joan Wistowe, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato John Sutton nel 1492, nel Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1498, all'età di 26 anni.
